imaging_findings: MRI of the lumbar spine shows post-laminectomy syndrome with persistent radicular pain despite surgical intervention.
functional_impairment: Significant pain with weight-bearing, limiting ability to work and perform household tasks.
clinical_notes_text: Patient DEMO-001, a 65-year-old male, presents with chronic spinal cord stimulator trial pain, reporting a symptom duration of 15 months. The patient describes significant pain with weight-bearing, limiting ability to work and perform household tasks.. Prior to this visit, the patient has undergone conservative management including 6 weeks of physical therapy, NSAIDs for 2 months. Recent imaging, an MRI performed on 2026-02-19, revealed post-laminectomy syndrome with persistent radicular pain despite surgical intervention.. The diagnosis is G89.4. Given the persistent symptoms, failed conservative treatments, and objective imaging findings, the patient is a candidate for spinal cord stimulator trial (CPT: 63650). Discussion with the patient confirms understanding of the procedure and risks. The patient's payer is Aetna. The current readiness score for prior authorization is green due to all criteria met for prior authorization..
